profile.name: Vashisth Goswami
profile.title: Cloud Support Engineer II - DevOps @ AWS
profile.tagline: Helping enterprises run reliable, automated, cost-aware infrastructure on AWS - containers, CI/CD, and Infrastructure as Code.

about: Cloud Support Engineer on the DevOps side at AWS, providing expert-level support for EC2, EKS, ECS, Lambda, S3, CloudFormation, and IAM. I work across development and operations teams to optimize CI/CD pipelines, troubleshoot container orchestration, and keep enterprise workloads highly available. I hold an M.S. in Information Systems (Data Science specialization) and enjoy turning messy infrastructure problems into automated, repeatable, cost-effective solutions.

skills.items: AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, EKS, ECS, Lambda, IAM, CloudFormation), GCP, Azure
Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, Spinnaker, Argo CD, OpenLens
Jenkins, Terraform, Ansible, Chef, Azure DevOps (VSTS), CodePipeline
Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, Splunk, New Relic, Nagios
Java, Python, C/C++, PL/SQL, JavaScript (Node.js, React, Express)
NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-Learn, Jupyter, Tableau, Weka
Git, GitHub, GitLab, Postman, PyCharm, IntelliJ, Linux

experience.note: Promoted to Cloud Support Engineer II - DevOps (Jul 2026) · Cloud Support Engineer I - DevOps (Jan 2024 - Jul 2026)
points: Provide expert-level support for AWS services (EC2, EKS, ECS, Lambda, S3, CloudFormation, IAM), ensuring high availability and efficient troubleshooting for enterprise clients in DevOps environments.
Collaborate with development and operations teams to optimize CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Git, and CodePipeline - improving deployment efficiency and reducing release-cycle times.
Resolve complex infrastructure and automation issues across Linux servers, networking, and container orchestration (Docker/Kubernetes), improving system stability and customer satisfaction.
Built and maintained highly scalable, fault-tolerant multi-tier Azure environments across multiple availability zones using Terraform and CloudFormation.
Deployed Java microservices via Azure DevOps and Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) managed clusters; exposed services through the Azure External Load Balancer.
Set up, upgraded, and maintained Kubernetes clusters for on-prem and cloud; managed containerized apps via nodes, ConfigMaps, Selectors, Services, and Pods.
Led part of the release-management team, containerizing applications and deploying to public and private clouds.
Implemented Redux for global application state management (e.g., user login status).
Automated Kubernetes cluster provisioning with Ansible playbooks; orchestrated Linux containers with Docker, Kubernetes, and CoreOS.
Built monitoring and metrics with Prometheus and Grafana, integrated with Slack; managed reproducible builds and Helm package releases.

projects.name: ShopFront on ECS Fargate
projects.desc: Containerized microservice storefront on Amazon ECS Fargate behind an ALB and CloudFront (HTTPS), with autoscaling, ECR, and least-privilege IAM. ~$26/mo.

projects.name: Orders Platform on EKS
projects.desc: Kubernetes platform on Amazon EKS - Deployments, Services, ALB Ingress, Horizontal Pod Autoscaler, and IRSA. Run as a cost-managed ephemeral environment.

projects.name: CI/CD Pipeline
projects.desc: GitHub Actions → Amazon ECS with keyless OIDC authentication, least-privilege deploy role, and health-gated rolling deployments.

academicProjects.name: Web Mining in the World Wide Web: A Survey
academicProjects.desc: Research paper surveying the evolution of web mining and how data-mining techniques have been adapted since their foundation.

academicProjects.name: Citizen's Attribute
academicProjects.desc: Application (Python & Java) to manage medical reports and coordinate with doctors, including a symptom-collecting chatbot that suggests guidance from a patient's records.

academicProjects.name: Hotel Booking System
academicProjects.desc: Booking system built in PL/SQL - dataset created with SQL and executed via stored procedures.

education.school: University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMBC) - Baltimore, MD
education.degree: Master of Science in Information Systems
